title: Excretions from the Two Openings
short_rule: Anything that exits from the front or rear passages nullifies Wudu.
details: Wudu is nullified by anything that exits from the two openings (penis/vagina, and anus). This includes the appearance of filth at the tip or edge of the opening.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Flowing Filth from the Body
short_rule: Filth like blood or pus that flows from a wound nullifies Wudu.
details: Filth that flows from other than the two openings, such as blood or pus, nullifies Wudu. The filth must physically flow beyond the point of exit to a place that normally must be cleaned.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Vomiting a Mouthful
short_rule: Vomiting a mouthful or more nullifies Wudu.
details: Vomiting an amount equal to a mouthful or more breaks one's Wudu. A mouthful is defined as an amount the mouth cannot withhold without strain.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Sleeping in an Unsupported Position
short_rule: Sleeping without the buttocks firmly planted on the ground nullifies Wudu.
details: Sleeping in a position such that the buttocks are not firmly planted onto the ground, such as while lying down or leaning on one's side, nullifies Wudu.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Loss of Consciousness or Sanity
short_rule: Loss of consciousness, insanity, or drunkenness completely nullifies Wudu.
details: Wudu is completely invalidated by the loss of consciousness, insanity, or drunkenness.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Laughing Out Loud in Prayer
short_rule: An adult laughing out loud during the ritual prayer nullifies their Wudu.
details: Laughing out loud, by an adult, while performing the ritual prayer (salat) nullifies ablution.
remedy_or_consequence: Must perform Wudu again and repeat the prayer.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Direct Contact between Genitalia
short_rule: Contact between an erect penis and vagina without a thick barrier nullifies Wudu.
details: Direct contact between an erect penis and vagina without a thick cloth acting as a barrier breaks the ablution. Direct skin contact elsewhere does not.
author: Abu 'l-Ikhlas al-Shurunbulali

title: Normal Excretions
short_rule: Urinating, flatulating, and the ejection of feces break Wudu.
details: The regular passing of urine, feces, and flatulence are fundamental acts that break ablution.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Uncontrolled & Other Emissions
short_rule: Pre-seminal discharge, post-urinary discharge, and rare uncontrolled emissions break Wudu.
details: Ablution is broken by uncontrolled emissions when they occur rarely, as well as pre-seminal discharge and post-urinary discharge.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Loss of Intellect
short_rule: Heavy sleep, drunkenness, fainting, or possession by jinn breaks Wudu.
details: The loss of normal intellect through heavy sleep, drunkenness, fainting, or possession by jinn breaks the state of ablution.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Lustful Physical Contact
short_rule: Caressing or kissing with normal sensual pleasure or desiring such pleasure breaks Wudu.
details: Caressing and kissing break Wudu if one finds normal sensual pleasure in it or if one desires such pleasure during the act.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Direct Contact with Genitalia
short_rule: A man touching his penis or a woman gently inserting her fingers into her vagina breaks Wudu.
details: Ablution is broken by direct contact with the genitalia, such as a woman gently inserting her finger(s) or hand in her vagina, or a man touching his penis.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Doubt Regarding Purity
short_rule: Doubting whether one has committed an act that breaks purity nullifies Wudu.
details: Simply doubting about committing an act which makes one lose pureness is sufficient to break the ablution.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Apostasy
short_rule: Disbelieving and becoming an apostate breaks ritual purity.
details: Disbelieving for he who becomes an apostate after being Muslim breaks the state of ablution.
remedy_or_consequence: Must perform Wudu upon returning to Islam.
author: Ibn 'Ashir

title: Exits from Private Parts
short_rule: Whatever exits from the private parts nullifies Wudu.
details: Wudu is nullified by what exits from the private parts. For the majority of Hanbalis, this even includes worms and stones exiting the passages.
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Voluminous Impure Discharges
short_rule: Impure discharges like blood or pus from elsewhere in the body nullify Wudu if they are voluminous.
details: Impure discharges from elsewhere in the body, such as blood, pus (qayh), and serosanguineous discharge (sadeed), nullify Wudu if they are voluminous enough to be repulsing.
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Loss of Intellect
short_rule: Loss of intellect nullifies Wudu, except for light sleep while sitting or standing.
details: Loss of intellect, including sleep, coma, insanity, or intoxication, nullifies Wudu. An exception is made for light sleep while sitting or standing.
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Touching the Genitals
short_rule: Touching the penis or female external sexual organs with one's hands nullifies Wudu.
details: Touching the penis with one's hands nullifies Wudu. This rule also applies to touching a woman's external sexual organs, whether touching one's own or another's.
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Lustful Skin Contact with Women
short_rule: A man's skin touching the skin of a woman with lustful desire nullifies Wudu.
details: If a man's skin touches the skin of a woman with lustful desire, it nullifies his Wudu.
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Apostasy from Islam
short_rule: Apostasy from Islam completely nullifies Wudu.
details: Renouncing Islam and becoming an apostate nullifies minor ablution.
remedy_or_consequence: Must perform Wudu again upon returning to Islam.
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi

title: Eating Camel Meat
short_rule: Consuming the meat of a camel nullifies Wudu.
details: Eating camel's meat nullifies Wudu. This is a distinctive position (mufradat) of the Hanbali school, based on a specific prophetic command.
author: Ibn Qudamah al-Maqdisi
